source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Nitrogen Cylinder Rupture

context.operator: Kosmos Energy Gulf of Mexico Operations

actions_taken: Safety stand-down initiated; all operations suspended
General alarm sounded; personnel mustered at designated point
Airgas HAZMAT response team vented cylinders after risk assessments and JSA
Onsite investigation conducted by BSEE on June 10, 2024
Plan to install nitrogen generation system in 2025 budget
Plan to install physical barrier and hard piping
event.summary: On June 5, 2024, at approximately 15:38 hours, a nitrogen cylinder ruptured inside a twelve-cylinder bottle rack aboard the Helix Q5000 during workover operations. The rupture caused damage to other bottles in the rack and scattered them across the main deck. Three personnel sustained minor injuries from shrapnel. The root cause was corrosion of the cylinder wall due to inadequate paint coating protection in the marine environment, combined with design deficiencies including lack of protective barriers between cylinders and rack, and inadequate drainage. Visual pre-fill inspections prior to offshore deployment did not identify the corrosion because the rack was not disassembled for thorough inspection of individual cylinders.
recommendations: BSEE recommends the Office of Incident Investigations consider a Safety Alert to warn industry about the risk that these bottle racks pose
Install nitrogen generation system to eliminate reliance on external rental systems
Install physical barrier and hard piping to mitigate exposure to personnel and equipment during potential future failures
Change vendor and adhere to elevated inspection process informed by lessons learned

threats.name: Cylinder wall corrosion due to inadequate paint coating
Metal-to-metal contact between cylinders and rack
Moisture accumulation due to lack of drainage in rack design

consequences.name: Operational shutdown and loss of production

controls.name: DOT special permit regulations requiring 10-year hydrostatic or ultrasonic examination
controls.barrier_role: Inspection and certification of cylinder integrity

supporting_text: the nitrogen cylinder rack adhered to Department of Transportation (DOT) special permit regulations, which mandate a 10-year hydrostatic or ultrasonic examination. However, these inspections failed to identify critical issues such as excessive corrosion with the internal cylinders

controls.name: Visual pre-fill inspections prior to offshore deployment
controls.barrier_role: Detection of visible defects before deployment

supporting_text: visual pre-fill inspections were performed prior to deployment of the bottles to offshore locations, they did not involve disassembly of the rack for a comprehensive evaluation of the individual cylinders and manifold components

Visual pre-fill inspections were conducted prior to mobilization offshore, but the rack was not disassembled for a thorough inspection of the individual cylinders or manifold components. This type of inspection did not allow for the bottom of the cylinders to be inspected for corrosion

The water-based paint coating on the cylinders failed to provide adequate corrosion protection in the marine environment the bottles experienced during their time offshore, accelerating corrosion over time
The water-based paint coating on the cylinders did not effectively prevent corrosion in the marine environment. The inadequate integrity of the cylinder coating allowed for corrosion over time

controls.name: Protective barrier between cylinders and rack
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of metal-to-metal contact and damage
controls.lod_basis: Design feature to prevent mechanical damage

supporting_text: the absence of a material barrier between the cylinders and the rack presents several significant safety concerns. This can include dents, scratches, or other forms of wear that may compromise the integrity of the cylinder, potentially leading to leaks
the lack of a protective barrier in the rack's design allowed for metal-to-metal contact between the bottles and the rack, increasing the risk of damage and eventually failure

controls.name: Drainage system in rack design
controls.barrier_role: Prevention of moisture accumulation and corrosion
controls.lod_basis: Design feature to manage moisture in marine environment

supporting_text: The lack of drainage in the rack's design left the cylinders exposed to an even greater corrosive environment than the already corrosive marine environment the bottles routinely experienced
The rack does not allow for proper drainage, which caused prolonged exposure of the cylinders to corrosive elements

controls.name: Toolbox talk and risk assessment prior to operations

supporting_text: 12:00 – 12:15: Subsea and Helix Control System (HCS) conducted toolbox talk and risk assessment

controls.name: Safety stand-down and general alarm activation
controls.barrier_role: Immediate response to incident and personnel protection

supporting_text: 16:14: Safety Standdown initiated; all operations suspended
16:41: General Alarm was sounded; a public announcement was made for personnel to muster at the designated muster point. Full muster recorded at 16:54

Three personnel in the area, staging equipment in preparation for pre-charging, sustained minor injuries from the shrapnel produced by the rupturing of the nitrogen bottle; all three personnel were evaluated by the rig medic and subsequently cleared to return to work

controls.name: HAZMAT response team venting of cylinders

supporting_text: 22:16: After risk assessments and job safety analysis (JSA) were completed, the Airgas HAZMAT response team began venting cylinders

controls.name: Installation of nitrogen generation system
controls.barrier_role: Elimination of external cylinder dependency and associated risks
controls.lod_basis: Planned system replacement to eliminate hazard source

supporting_text: The operator plans to install a nitrogen generation system to eliminate the reliance on external rental systems. The transition to a nitrogen generation system, which will be included in the 2025 budget

controls.name: Installation of physical barrier and hard piping
controls.barrier_role: Containment and personnel protection from projectiles
controls.lod_basis: Planned engineering control to mitigate consequences

supporting_text: Helix plans to install a physical barrier and hard piping to mitigate exposure to personnel and equipment during potential future failures, thereby ensuring a safer working environment

controls.name: Vendor change and elevated inspection process
controls.barrier_role: Quality assurance and improved supplier management
controls.lod_basis: Supplier quality control and inspection standards

supporting_text: Helix elected to change their vendor as one of the corrective actions, demonstrating Helix's commitment to accountability and quality assurance. The selection of a new vendor and adhering to an elevated inspection process informed by lessons learned from this incident

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
